implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-001: Docker Compose configuration with pgvector/pgvector:pg16 and dynamic port binding
AC-003: Docker availability check with fast-fail
AC-004: Session-scoped ephemeral_pg fixture with random port and cleanup
AC-005: pgvector extension verification after startup
AC-006: Parallel worktree isolation via ephemeral_pg_factory
AC-007: Deterministic fake_embed fixture with no external dependencies

completion_promises.criterion_text: deploy/local/docker-compose.yml exists: image pgvector/pgvector:pg16, host port env-overridable (no fixed 5432 binding; fixture assigns a free random port), mounts ./initdb to /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d, defines a pg_isready healthcheck with interval: 2s, retries: 10
completion_promises.evidence: File exists at deploy/local/docker-compose.yml. Verified contents: uses pgvector/pgvector:pg16 image (line 3), PGPORT env var with ${PGPORT:-5432} for dynamic port configuration with no fixed binding (lines 8, 10), volumes mount ./initdb to /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d (line 12), healthcheck with pg_isready command (line 14), interval 2s (line 15), retries 10 (line 17), timeout 5s (line 16), start_period 5s (line 18). Integration tests verify random port allocation and fixture functionality.

completion_promises.criterion_text: deploy/local/initdb/01_extensions.sql contains CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;
completion_promises.evidence: File exists at deploy/local/initdb/01_extensions.sql. Verified contents: line 2 contains exactly 'CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;' with proper SQL syntax. This script is mounted to /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d in docker-compose.yml and executed automatically on container initialization.

completion_promises.criterion_text: With Docker unavailable, an explicit python -m pytest tests/integration/ -m integration run fails/skips quickly (no hang) with a message naming Docker / the container runtime as the missing prerequisite
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented in tests/integration/conftest.py lines 106-115. The _is_docker_available() function checks Docker availability via 'docker info' subprocess call with 5-second timeout. The ephemeral_pg fixture calls this check at startup and calls pytest.skip() with message 'Docker is not available. Integration tests require Docker or a compatible container runtime. Install Docker Desktop or configure your container runtime.' This ensures immediate skip with no hang when Docker is unavailable.

completion_promises.criterion_text: The session-scoped ephemeral_pg fixture yields a plain postgresql:// DSN at 127.0.0.1 on a non-5432 random port; after session teardown docker compose -p <project> ps -q is empty and the anonymous volume is removed (down -v) — verified by a minimal integration self-test
completion_promises.evidence: Fixture implementation in tests/integration/conftest.py lines 95-180. Random port allocation via _get_random_port() function (lines 30-40) using socket binding to port 0. DSN format 'postgresql://fleet_memory:fleet_memory@127.0.0.1:{port}/fleet_memory' (line 168). Teardown via _teardown_compose() function (lines 76-92) calling 'docker compose down -v --remove-orphans' to remove containers and volumes. Registered via request.addfinalizer (line 174) and atexit.register (line 175) for cleanup on both normal exit and aborted runs. Verified by test_ephemeral_pg_starts_and_provides_dsn (PASSED), test_ephemeral_pg_uses_random_port (PASSED), test_teardown_removes_container_and_volume (PASSED).

completion_promises.criterion_text: After fixture startup, SELECT extname FROM pg_extension includes vector (init script ran on the fresh volume — review risk R3)
completion_promises.evidence: Verified by test_pgvector_extension_available in tests/integration/test_ephemeral_fixture.py (PASSED in 13.52s test run). Test connects to ephemeral_pg DSN via psycopg, executes 'SELECT extname FROM pg_extension WHERE extname = 'vector'', and asserts result is not None and equals 'vector'. Test passing confirms that the init script at deploy/local/initdb/01_extensions.sql was executed successfully during container initialization and the pgvector extension is installed and available.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Two fixture instantiations with distinct project uids yield DSNs on different ports with mutually invisible data (parallel-isolation self-test)
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented ephemeral_pg_factory fixture in tests/integration/conftest.py lines 183-273. Factory creates independent PostgreSQL instances with unique UUID-based project names (line 191). Verified by test_data_isolation_between_projects (PASSED). Test creates table 'test_table' in first instance, spawns second instance via factory with different project_uid, extracts ports from both DSNs and confirms they differ, then queries information_schema.tables in second instance and confirms it has no visibility to the first instance's 'test_table'. Test passing confirms complete data isolation between parallel worktree instances.

completion_promises.criterion_text: tests/conftest.py exports a fake_embed fixture whose fake is defined INLINE (deterministic 768-dim vectors, no import from fleet_memory.embed — that module lands in a later wave); using it alone opens no database or network connection
completion_promises.evidence: File exists at tests/conftest.py. Verified implementation: @pytest.fixture fake_embed at line 18 returns Callable[[str], list[float]]. Implementation is fully inline using hash-seeded linear congruential generator (lines 34-50), generates exactly 768 dimensions (line 43 range), uses only stdlib imports (lines 8-15: annotations, typing, pytest). No imports from fleet_memory modules. Verified no network/database usage by unit tests: test_fake_embed_produces_768_dim_vector, test_fake_embed_is_deterministic, test_fake_embed_different_inputs_differ, test_fake_embed_values_in_valid_range - all PASSED in 0.04s with no external dependencies or I/O.
